caption: The video appears to be a screen recording of an IRS webpage discussing Required Minimum Distributions (RMDs) for retirement plans. The page explains that individuals over age 72 must start withdrawing funds from their IRAs and retirement plans by December 31, 2023, and each subsequent year. It details specific rules for various retirement plans such as 401(k), 403(b), and 457(b) plans, noting that the first RMD is due by April 1 of the year following the year they reach age 72 or leave employment, whichever is later. The video also mentions that RMDs cannot be rolled over to another IRA or retirement plan and highlights that Roth IRAs do not require distributions while the original owner is alive. Additionally, it introduces the topic of RMD calculations and the potential 50% tax penalty on missed distributions, indicating that further information will follow. The cursor is visible, suggesting that the viewer may be navigating through the content.
